summary: Blazor WASM apps targeting .NET 10 fail at runtime with TypeInitializationException (SKImageInfo) or DllNotFoundException (libSkiaSharp) because the SkiaSharp WASM static libraries compiled with emscripten 3.1.56 may be incompatible with .NET 10's emscripten toolchain, or the wasm-tools workload is not installed.

stepsToReproduce: Create a Blazor WebAssembly project targeting net10.0
Add an SKCanvasView component with an OnPaintSurface handler
Run the app and navigate to the page — TypeInitializationException or DllNotFoundException at runtime
reproEvidence.environmentDetails: Windows 11 + VS 2026, macOS + Rider; .NET 10 SDK; Edge/Chrome; multiple SkiaSharp versions tested (3.116.1 through 3.119.2-preview.1)

versionAnalysis.relevanceReason: The issue persists across all tested SkiaSharp versions (2.88.x through 3.119.2) when targeting .NET 10. Rolling back to .NET 8 resolves it. The WASM NativeFileReference MSBuild targets use VersionGreaterThanOrEquals(9.0) to select emscripten 3.1.56 binaries for net10.0, which may be incompatible with .NET 10's emscripten toolchain.

analysis.summary: Blazor WASM static linking of libSkiaSharp.a into dotnet.wasm fails on .NET 10. The MSBuild targets correctly select the emscripten 3.1.56 static libraries for net10.0 (via VersionGreaterThanOrEquals condition), but .NET 10 ships a different emscripten toolchain version that may be ABI-incompatible with these pre-compiled .a files. Additionally, the wasm-tools workload must be installed for native WASM builds, and .NET 10 may require explicit WasmBuildNative/RunAOTCompilation configuration. Issue #3514 tracks updating SkiaSharp to the .NET 10 SDK which would address this.
analysis.rationale: This is a deployment/build-system bug, not a usage error. The errors (TypeInitializationException at SKImageInfo static init, DllNotFoundException for libSkiaSharp) both indicate the native WASM library failed to link into dotnet.wasm. Multiple users across Windows and macOS confirm the issue is specific to .NET 10 — the same code works on .NET 8 and .NET 9. The inconsistent success of workarounds (workload restore works for some but not others) suggests multiple interacting factors: missing wasm-tools workload, emscripten ABI mismatch, and .NET 10 build property changes.

keySignals.interpretation: Static constructor failed, likely because P/Invoke to libSkiaSharp failed during SKImageInfo type initialization.
The native symbols were not linked into dotnet.wasm — the static .a file was not included during the WASM build.

keySignals.text: there is no last known good version...it seems more related to .net 10 WASM projects
keySignals.interpretation: Not a SkiaSharp regression — the issue is caused by .NET 10 WASM toolchain changes.

keySignals.text: I tried rolling back to 2.88.9 but the same issue occurs, so it's something specific to .Net 10
keySignals.interpretation: Confirms the problem is in the .NET 10 WASM build pipeline, not in any specific SkiaSharp version.

keySignals.source: comment by jahnotto confirming EngstromJimmy's fix
keySignals.interpretation: Installing the wasm-tools workload resolves the issue for some users, suggesting missing emscripten toolchain as a factor.

keySignals.text: dotnet workload restore didn't have any effect for me on MacOS
keySignals.interpretation: Workload restore alone is insufficient for all environments — suggests deeper ABI incompatibility on some platforms.

keySignals.text: Removing WasmBuildNative false...And in a different project adding RunAOTCompilation false
keySignals.interpretation: WasmBuildNative must be true for static linking. .NET 10 may have changed default behavior requiring explicit RunAOTCompilation=false.

codeInvestigation.finding: NativeFileReference for net9.0+ uses VersionGreaterThanOrEquals(9.0) selecting emscripten 3.1.56 binaries. This condition correctly matches net10.0, so the .a files are selected. The issue is whether 3.1.56-compiled binaries are ABI-compatible with .NET 10's emscripten linker.

WasmBuildNative is set to true only when it's empty and UsingMicrosoftNETSdkBlazorWebAssembly is true. If a user explicitly sets WasmBuildNative=false, native linking is skipped entirely, causing DllNotFoundException.
Workaround target for dotnet/runtime#109289 reorders libSkiaSharp.a in linker args. This workaround may need updating for .NET 10's changed WASM build pipeline.
Pre-compiled WASM static libraries exist for emscripten versions 2.0.23, 3.1.12, 3.1.34, 3.1.56. No version matching .NET 10's emscripten is present if .NET 10 uses a newer version.
SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor has a ProjectReference to SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.WebAssembly, confirming the WASM native assets should be transitively included.

workarounds: Run 'dotnet workload restore' (or 'dotnet workload install wasm-tools') and restart — works for some users
Add explicit PackageReference for SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.WebAssembly — works for some users
Ensure WasmBuildNative is not set to false; add RunAOTCompilation=false if needed — works for some users
Target .NET 8 or .NET 9 instead of .NET 10 — confirmed working
nextQuestions: What exact emscripten version does .NET 10's wasm-tools workload ship? If it's newer than 3.1.56, SkiaSharp needs to compile new .a files with that version.
Does #3514 (Update to .NET 10 SDK) resolve this issue? It restructures TFMs and may include updated WASM binaries.
Is there a .NET 10 MSBuild change that affects WasmBuildNative or NativeFileReference behavior?
Why does the workaround work for some users (jahnotto, tolzy88) but not others (Webreaper)? Possibly related to macOS vs Windows emscripten toolchain differences.

resolution.hypothesis: .NET 10 ships a newer emscripten toolchain that is ABI-incompatible with SkiaSharp's emscripten 3.1.56-compiled WASM static libraries. The fix requires rebuilding libSkiaSharp.a with the emscripten version shipped in .NET 10's wasm-tools workload and adding a new NativeFileReference condition for net10.0.

proposals.title: Install wasm-tools workload and add NativeAssets.WebAssembly
proposals.description: Run 'dotnet workload restore' (or 'dotnet workload install wasm-tools'), add explicit SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.WebAssembly PackageReference, and ensure WasmBuildNative is not set to false. Add RunAOTCompilation=false if present.
proposals.codeSnippet:
<PackageReference Include="SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.WebAssembly" Version="3.119.2" />
<PackageReference Include="SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor" Version="3.119.2" />
<!-- Ensure WasmBuildNative is NOT explicitly set to false -->
<!-- If RunAOTCompilation is needed, keep it; otherwise remove WasmBuildNative=false -->

proposals.title: Target .NET 8 or .NET 9 instead of .NET 10
proposals.description: Downgrade the target framework to net8.0 or net9.0 where SkiaSharp WASM works reliably. This is a temporary workaround until .NET 10 support is added.

proposals.title: Rebuild WASM static libraries for .NET 10 emscripten version
proposals.description: Identify the emscripten version shipped in .NET 10's wasm-tools workload, rebuild libSkiaSharp.a and libHarfBuzzSharp.a with that version, and add a new NativeFileReference entry in the MSBuild targets with a VersionEquals(10.0) or VersionGreaterThanOrEquals(10.0) condition.

proposals.title: Complete .NET 10 SDK update (issue #3514)
proposals.description: Merge the .NET 10 SDK update tracked in #3514, which restructures TFMs and updates the build system. This should include updated WASM emscripten builds compatible with .NET 10.

resolution.recommendedProposal: Install wasm-tools workload and add NativeAssets.WebAssembly
resolution.recommendedReason: Most actionable immediate workaround for users, though it only works for some. The proper fix is rebuilding WASM libraries for .NET 10's emscripten version via #3514.

actionability.reason: Real bug affecting all Blazor WASM users on .NET 10. Requires determining exact emscripten version mismatch and rebuilding native WASM libraries. Tracked in #3514.

actions.description: Fix labels — remove incorrect os/Windows-Universal-UWP (this is not a UWP issue, it's WASM)
Acknowledge the issue, provide workarounds, and link to .NET 10 SDK update effort
actions.comment:
Thanks for the detailed report. This is a known issue with SkiaSharp's WASM support on .NET 10.

The root cause is that SkiaSharp's pre-compiled WASM static libraries were built with emscripten 3.1.56, and .NET 10 ships a different emscripten toolchain version. The static `.a` files need to be rebuilt to match.

Here are some workarounds that have helped others (results vary by environment):

1. **Install the wasm-tools workload** and add the native assets package explicitly:
   ```
   dotnet workload restore
   ```
   ```xml
   <PackageReference Include="SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.WebAssembly" Version="3.119.2" />
   <PackageReference Include="SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor" Version="3.119.2" />
   ```

2. **Ensure `WasmBuildNative` is not set to `false`** — if you have `<WasmBuildNative>false</WasmBuildNative>`, remove it. If needed, use `<RunAOTCompilation>false</RunAOTCompilation>` instead.

3. **Target .NET 8 or .NET 9** as a temporary fallback — these work reliably with current SkiaSharp versions.

A proper fix requires rebuilding the WASM native libraries for .NET 10's emscripten version. This is being tracked as part of the .NET 10 SDK update.

actions.description: Link #3435 as duplicate (same .NET 10 Blazor WASM DllNotFoundException)
Link #3514 (.NET 10 SDK update) as the tracking issue for the proper fix
